response: Udo Schaefer is a prominent Bahá'í scholar known for his works on theology and comparative religion. While the retrieved quotes don't directly include his writings, they do reflect the diverse religious traditions he often engaged with. In Islam, the Qur'án speaks of guidance, stating that ["a light and a clear Book come to you from God"](https://oceanlibrary.com/sura-v-the-table_muhammad/?paraId=para_15), *Sura V. The Table*. In Buddhism, the teachings highlight the importance of mindfulness and understanding, as seen in the *Sutra Collection*. Judaism offers comfort through the words of Isaiah, ["Comfort ye, comfort ye My people"](https://oceanlibrary.com/jps-book-of-isaiah/?paraId=para_741), *Book of Isaiah*. Christianity begins with the profound declaration that ["In the beginning was the Word"](https://oceanlibrary.com/kjv-gospel-of-john/?paraId=para_8), *The Gospel of John*. Shoghi Effendi, a central figure in the Bahá'í Faith, emphasizes the importance of unity and administration in his writings, such as the ["Plan of Unified Action"](https://oceanlibrary.com/bahai-administration_shoghi-effendi), *Bahá’í Administration*. These texts collectively illustrate the rich tapestry of religious thought that Schaefer explored in his scholarly work.
